calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-06-2022 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Jason M. Richardson, Esq., appearing for defendant. No appearance by the plaintiff. Notice having been given and there being no appearance by plaintiff, the court orders the case dismissed pursuant to Government Code section 68608(b). The court finds no good cause or substantial justification for failure to comply with the previous court orders. A less severe sanction would not be effective due to the history of lack of compliance.
Judge Samuel K. Feng; Clerk VWU; Not Reported. (610)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-29-2020 Continued To Mar-02-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Dec-21-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via ZOOM Video-conferencing:
Attorney, Nalani L. Crisologo (SBN 313402) of the Law Offices of Shook, Hardy & Bacon L.L.P. (Tel: Tel: 415.544.1900, Email: ncrisologo@shb.com) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter.

Defendant stipulates to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The judge Pro Tem and counsel authorize the court clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

There being no opposition, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT JUAN CASTILLO'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities is GRANTED in its entirety. Sanctions of $1,380 are awarded. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the adopted ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge Pro Tem: Bruce Highman: Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: not reported (Disc302)
